_comment: Profile schema for kernel_vr_skills MAP stage. This file is GENERATED by the PROFILE step (an LLM subagent reads the target and fills surface/indicator patterns). The out_of_scope block is filled by a HUMAN after reading out/scope_proposal.md. vr_map.py consumes this file. All *_patterns values are Python regular expressions (re module syntax).
description: One-line description of what this driver/subsystem is.
_surface_patterns_comment: Content regexes that mark where a user-reachable entrypoint is REGISTERED. When one matches a line, the enclosing function becomes an entrypoint candidate.
_entrypoint_name_patterns_comment: Function-NAME regexes. A function whose name matches is treated as an entrypoint even if no surface_pattern line matched.
_indicator_patterns_comment: Patterns that raise/lower a function's suspicion score. user_control/lifetime/concurrency raise it; guards are tracked for context.
_out_of_scope_comment: DEFAULTS TO EMPTY — nothing is excluded unless a HUMAN adds it after reading out/scope_proposal.md. Possible values: surface_types e.g. 'debugfs'|'sysfs'|'module_param' (drop entrypoints of these kinds); require_privilege=true (drop functions behind capable()/CAP_*); config_gates e.g. 'CONFIG_MALI_DEBUG' (drop functions under these #ifdef gates); path_excludes e.g. '*test*' (glob path filters); impact_classes e.g. 'dos'|'info_leak' enforced in CLASSIFY. NOTE: DoS is a VALID vulnerability by default — only put 'dos' here if YOU decide DoS-only bugs are not in scope for this engagement. Anything excluded is logged with a reason (out/excluded.json for MAP, out_of_scope bucket in classified.json for CLASSIFY), never silently.
